name: Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail
name_original: La Fortaleza de Ledesma y el Puente Mocho
description: Hike the easy Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail, a 4.9-mile route through *dehesa* landscape to a historic Roman bridge.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Ledesma traverse a diverse natural landscape characterized by the Ledesma River and extensive dehesa woodlands, primarily composed of holm oaks. The region's altitude ranges from 700 to 800 meters, offering varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Historic structures, including ancient bridges and a castle, are integrated into many routes, providing cultural points of interest. Best hiking trails around Ledesma The most popular hiking route is Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route traverses the dehesa landscape and leads to a historic Roman bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Old Baths Trail Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.6 km) path. This circular trail connects Juzbado to Balneario de Ledesma, showcasing dehesas, river views, and historical paths. Local hikers also love the Ledesma Historic Trail, a 1.3 miles (2.2 km) trail leading through the historic town, passing landmarks such as the castle and the Church of Santa María la Mayor, often completed in about 36 minutes. Hiking around Ledesma is defined by dehesa landscapes, river valleys, and historic townscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Ledesma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 5 reviews. More than 29 hikers have used komoot to explore Ledesma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ledesma?
A: Ledesma offers several hiking trails, with four distinct routes detailed in this guide. These range from easy strolls through the historic town to moderate excursions across the dehesa landscape and along river views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Ledesma?
A: Hiking trails around Ledesma primarily feature a diverse natural landscape characterized by the scenic Ledesma River and extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; woodlands, predominantly composed of holm oaks. You'll encounter varied terrain with altitudes ranging from 700 to 800 meters, often including historical paths and river valleys.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Ledesma?
A: Yes, Ledesma has easy trails perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208641121/ledesma-historic-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma Historic Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.2 km route that takes you through the historic town, passing landmarks like the castle and the Church of Santa María la Mayor. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993164045/puente-mocho-and-calzada-romana-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 km long and traverses the dehesa landscape to a historic Roman bridge.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while hiking in Ledesma?
A: Ledesma's trails are rich in both history and nature. You can explore ancient structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho&lt;/a&gt;, a historic Roman bridge, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fortaleza - Ledesma Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5574008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma Bridge over the Tormes River&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark. Natural features include the Ledesma River and the unique &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape with its holm oaks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Ledesma area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210322830/old-baths-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Baths Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6 km circular trail that connects Juzbado to Balneario de Ledesma, offering scenic dehesas, river views, and historical paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208641121/ledesma-historic-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma Historic Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a shorter, easy circular option within the town.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ledesma?
A: The best time to enjoy hiking and other outdoor activities in Ledesma is during spring and autumn. The weather during these seasons is typically milder and more pleasant, making for ideal conditions to explore the trails and natural landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ledesma?
A: The hiking routes in Ledesma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the integration of historical sites, and the scenic beauty of the dehesa landscapes and river views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ledesma?
A: For families, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208641121/ledesma-historic-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma Historic Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's an easy 2.2 km circular route through the town, allowing families to combine a gentle walk with cultural exploration of landmarks like the castle and churches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993164045/puente-mocho-and-calzada-romana-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also an easy option, offering a pleasant walk through nature and history.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ledesma?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on each trail are not detailed, generally, trails in natural areas like the &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape are suitable for dogs, provided they are kept under control. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific trail rules for any restrictions, especially in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in Ledesma?
A: The trails in this guide for Ledesma are categorized as easy to moderate. There are no routes classified as difficult. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210322830/old-baths-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Baths Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Trail – Almenara de Tormes loop from Juzbado&lt;/a&gt;, offer longer distances and some elevation changes, providing a good workout without being overly strenuous.

Q: What makes the dehesa landscape unique for hiking in Ledesma?
A: The &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape around Ledesma is a unique ecological setting characterized by open woodlands of holm oaks. This traditional agricultural system supports diverse flora and fauna, offering hikers a distinct environment that combines natural beauty with a sense of cultural heritage. It's a landscape that is both scenic and historically significant.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available for accessing the trailheads?
A: While specific public transport details to individual trailheads are not readily available, Ledesma is a town with general transport links. For those driving, parking is typically available within or near the town center, from where many trails, especially the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208641121/ledesma-historic-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma Historic Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are easily accessible. For routes further afield, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993164045/puente-mocho-and-calzada-romana-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail&lt;/a&gt;, driving to a nearby starting point is often the most convenient option.
tips: This bridge, cataloged as an Asset of Cultural Interest in the category of Historic-Artistic Monument together with the remains of the Roman road at its ends, is considered by specialists to be of Roman origin, although from those times some think that only the site and the foundations that support it.

Ledesma still preserves a large part of the granite stone wall that has historically surrounded the Villa in its entirety. Most of what remains today was built in the time of Fernando II de León, in the 12th century, although it was in the 15th century when several canvases with well-carved masonry were renovated.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered very easy, making it suitable for hikers of all ages, including families with children. It's also suitable for cycling.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows a dirt path through a beautiful dehesa landscape, characterized by ancient holm oak trees and large rocks. Near the Puente Mocho, the landscape becomes more rocky with vegetation like broom, rockrose, and thyme. The Roman road sections feature irregular granite slabs, with larger stones at the sides to improve grip.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the Puente Mocho itself, an ancient bridge with Roman origins and medieval reforms. You'll also walk along well-preserved sections of the Roman road. The trail traverses a unique dehesa ecosystem with centenary holm oaks and offers picturesque views of the Cañedo river valley. Keep an eye out for wildlife such as griffon vultures, black kites, and hares.

faqs.question: Is the Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! Given its very easy difficulty rating and relatively short distance of 7.8 km, it's an excellent choice for families with children. The varied scenery and historical elements provide engaging points of interest for all.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, trails in natural areas like the dehesa are generally dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect other hikers. Remember to bring water for your pet and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally the best times. During these seasons, the weather is milder, the landscape is vibrant, and you can avoid the intense heat of summer. Winter can also be pleasant, but check local weather forecasts for rain or colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Ledesma, Salamanca. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, it's common for trails of this nature to have parking available near the start point, often in or around the nearest village. We recommend checking local maps or Ledesma's municipal information for designated parking spots.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including significant portions of the Calzada Romana (Roman Road) and a segment of the PuenteViejo trail.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Ledesma, a town that offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in Ledesma to enjoy before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot on the trail?
faqs.answer: The dehesa ecosystem is rich in biodiversity. Hikers have the chance to observe various bird species, including griffon vultures, black kites, and kestrels. Mammals like rabbits and hares are also common in the area.
